name: Citrus-Buttered Lobster Tails
recipeInstructions:
1. Place frozen lobster tails in a shallow baking dish.
2. Micro-cook, covered, on 30% power for 7 to 8 minutes or till thawed, rotating the dish a quarter-turn once. The tails are thawed when the shells are flexible enough to bend.
3. Using a heavy knife, cut through the center of the top shells. Continue cutting through the meat but not through the undershells.
4. Spread the tails open, butterfly style, so the meat is on top. Return them to the shallow baking dish.
5. Pour water atop. Micro-cook, covered, on 50% power for 6 to 8 minutes or just till the meat is opaque, rotating the dish a quarter-turn every minute. (Shield cooked meat with small pieces of foil, if necessary, to prevent overcooking.)
6. Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, combine butter or margarine, lemon juice, orange peel, salt, ginger, and paprika. Micro-cook, uncovered, on 100% power for 1 1/2 to 2 minutes or till the butter is melted. Mix well.
8. Drizzle lobster tails with butter mixture.
